general_notes: All values refer to the electronic ground state of the specified diatomic isotopologue. Vibrational wavenumber we (omega_e) is the harmonic vibrational frequency in cm^-1. Anharmonicity constant wexe (omega_e * x_e) is in cm^-1. Both are Dunham expansion coefficients. Authoritative source: NIST Chemistry WebBook / Huber & Herzberg 'Molecular Spectra and Molecular Structure IV' (1979) / Irikura JPCRD 2007. These are fixed, well-tabulated constants.

search_groups.description: Diatomic isotopologue in mass-number + element symbol notation.
Vibrational wavenumber and anharmonicity constant from NIST WebBook and Huber-Herzberg tables.

validation_targets.description: Diatomic isotopologue in mass-number + element symbol hyphenated notation.
validation_targets.notes: e.g. H-35Cl, H-79Br, 32S-16O. Specifies the exact isotopologue unambiguously.

validation_targets.description: Harmonic vibrational wavenumber omega_e for the ground electronic state in cm^-1.
validation_targets.notes: Source: NIST WebBook / Huber-Herzberg. Report to 3 decimal places for hydrogen-containing species, 1 decimal for heavier diatomics.

validation_targets.description: Anharmonicity constant omega_e * x_e for the ground electronic state in cm^-1.
validation_targets.notes: Source: NIST WebBook / Huber-Herzberg. Report to 3 decimal places for hydrogen-containing species, 2 decimal for heavier diatomics.
